references.title: Best VPN Service 2026
The Best VPNs
VPN Comparison 2026

en.commentary: I've tested every major VPN service in this ranking, and the single most important thing I can tell you about choosing a VPN is this: your threat model should determine your choice, ahead of marketing materials or speed tests. Mullvad is #1 because it has the most radical privacy architecture in the industry: anonymous accounts, zero email required, zero personal information collected, cash-in-an-envelope payment accepted. If a government subpoenas Mullvad, the result is an empty record, a technical fact about their data model and a structural guarantee. ProtonVPN at #2 is the choice for people who want Mullvad-level privacy principles with a more conventional user experience and the additional protection of Swiss law. Their open-source apps mean anyone can verify what the software is actually doing, a level of transparency that sets a higher bar for the entire category. NordVPN at #4 is an excellent product. Its privacy score of 8.0 reflects its Panama headquarters and holding company ownership structure, including a data breach incident in its history, which sophisticated privacy-conscious users weigh accordingly. Mullvad and ProtonVPN are the correct choices for privacy-critical use cases. NordVPN is the correct choice for streaming and general browsing. The Kape Technologies conflict deserves more attention than it gets: they now own ExpressVPN, Private Internet Access, and CyberGhost. Three competing VPNs under one parent company with a murky acquisition history is something privacy-conscious users should factor into their decision.

highlights.title: Mullvad Is #1 Because 'No Account Exists' Is a Technically Superior Privacy Model
highlights.body: Mullvad gives you a random 16-digit account number and that is all. When your subscription ends, the record is empty: zero account, zero profile, zero history. When law enforcement requests user data, Mullvad hands over an empty record. This is a privacy architecture, far beyond any policy. The difference is fundamental.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN's Open-Source Apps Are the Most Under-Appreciated Feature in This Entire Category
highlights.body: Proton publishes the source code for all of their VPN applications on GitHub. This means independent security researchers have verified that the apps do exactly what Proton claims: zero logging, zero beaconing, and zero hidden trackers. ProtonVPN gives you the source code to verify the claims yourself. That distinction matters enormously when your threat model includes well-funded adversaries.

highlights.title: NordVPN Is the Correct Choice for Streaming, Mullvad and ProtonVPN Are the Correct Choices for High-Stakes Privacy
highlights.body: NordVPN is a polished, fast, user-friendly service with 6,400+ servers. For unlocking streaming libraries, it's among the best. Its Panama jurisdiction, holding company ownership structure, and the 2018 data breach incident are background facts that sophisticated privacy-conscious users weigh. Mullvad and ProtonVPN deliver stronger structural privacy guarantees for those use cases. NordVPN is the correct tool for convenience and streaming. Mullvad is the correct tool for maximum protection.

highlights.title: Kape Technologies Owning Three VPNs Is the Most Underreported Story in This Category
highlights.body: ExpressVPN, Private Internet Access, and CyberGhost are all owned by Kape Technologies. These three services compete against each other in every VPN ranking, creating the illusion of diversity in the market. Kape's acquisition history, including the acquisition of Crossrider, which was previously known for distributing ad-injection malware, is something that any privacy-focused buyer should research before choosing any of these three services.

highlights.title: Windscribe's Free Tier Is the Most Defensible Free VPN Option Available
highlights.body: Windscribe's free tier operates as a genuine loss-leader for their paid product, with full transparency as a clean product trial. The R.O.B.E.R.T. DNS blocker works on the free tier. The privacy policy is honest. For light users who prefer to evaluate before committing to a paid subscription, Windscribe is the one free VPN I'm comfortable recommending.

zh-tw.commentary:
這個榜單裡的每款主流 VPN 我都測試過，關於選 VPN 最重要的一件事是：你的威脅模型決定你的選擇，行銷文案和速度測試只是次要參考。

Mullvad 排第一，這個結論我底氣十足。業界最徹底的隱私架構，匿名帳號制、零電子郵件要求、零個人資料收集、接受現金支付。政府傳喚 Mullvad，他們提交的是空的記錄，這是技術上的結構性保障，架構設計本身讓資料記錄在技術上根本無從發生。

ProtonVPN 排第二，是想要 Mullvad 等級隱私原則、同時要更友善介面和瑞士法律保護的正確選擇。開源應用讓任何人都能驗證軟體實際在做什麼，這個透明度在這類服務裡是真正稀有的。

NordVPN 第四名，產品本身很好，隱私評分 8.0 反映的是巴拿馬設籍加上 2018 年第三方洩露事件的背景，有意識的用戶知道這些事實後自然會有自己的判斷。Kape Technologies 同時持有 ExpressVPN、PIA 和 CyberGhost 三個相互競爭的 VPN，台灣在意隱私的用戶選購前可以了解這個背景。

highlights.title: Mullvad 排第一，「帳號根本不存在」是技術上更優的隱私模型
highlights.body: 這張榜單上其他每款 VPN 都要你提供電子郵件或帳號憑證。Mullvad 只給你一個隨機的 16 位帳號，就這樣。訂閱到期，記錄就是空的，帳號歸零、個人資料歸零、歷史歸零。執法機關要求用戶資料，Mullvad 交出空的記錄。這是隱私架構，遠超任何政策聲明。這個差異是根本性的，講這話我底氣十足。

highlights.title: ProtonVPN 的開源應用是這個品類裡最被低估的功能
highlights.body: Proton 把所有 VPN 應用的原始碼都公開在 GitHub 上。獨立安全研究員可以直接驗證這些應用做到了 Proton 說的，零日誌、零信標、零隱藏追蹤器。這個透明度在台灣遠端工作或隱私需求高的用戶選擇 VPN 時，是真正有意義的判斷依據。

highlights.title: NordVPN 串流表現很好，重度隱私需求的台灣用戶可以考慮 Mullvad 或 ProtonVPN
highlights.body: NordVPN 是精良、快速、介面友善的服務，6,400 多台伺服器，解鎖串流資料庫的能力在這個榜單裡數一數二。巴拿馬設籍、控股公司架構、2018 年第三方洩露事件，這些背景有意識的用戶應該知道。日常用途和串流用 NordVPN 沒問題，需要更高安全感的場景可以考慮 Mullvad。

highlights.title: Kape Technologies 同時持有三個 VPN，這件事在台灣 VPN 討論裡被低估了
highlights.body: ExpressVPN、Private Internet Access 和 CyberGhost 都是 Kape Technologies 持有的品牌。這三個服務在每個 VPN 評比裡相互競爭，製造市場多元的假象。Kape 的收購歷史包括買下曾以分發廣告注入惡意軟體聞名的 Crossrider，在意隱私的人在選這三款之前，背景值得查一查。

highlights.title: Windscribe 免費版是目前最值得推薦的免費 VPN 選項
highlights.body: Windscribe 的免費版定位清楚，是付費版的誠實試用入口，R.O.B.E.R.T. DNS 封鎖器在免費版上有效，隱私政策誠實。在台灣還在評估自己會不會真的用 VPN 的輕度用戶，Windscribe 是我會推薦的起點。

references.title: Top 9 VPNs of March 2026
Lawmakers question VPN impact on Americans' FISA surveillance protections
Legislation, loopholes, and loose ends — what does 2026 hold for the VPN industry?

en.commentary: March 30th brings two developments that directly validate why my top two rankings look the way they do. First: ProtonVPN completed its fourth consecutive no-logs audit and, crucially, its first SOC 2 Type II certification. This surpasses a standard no-logs audit. SOC 2 Type II requires auditors to observe the organization's controls over a sustained period, months rather than days, confirming that the privacy controls work consistently in practice rather than only during an audit window. I've raised ProtonVPN's privacy score to 9.6 accordingly, and it now ties Mullvad at 9.1 overall. Second: NordVPN has rolled out post-quantum encryption across all NordLynx connections on every platform. This is a genuine technical milestone: current encryption standards, while unbreakable today, are theoretically vulnerable to future quantum computers. NordVPN is the first mainstream VPN to address this threat systematically. The American political landscape is also shifting in ways that directly affect VPN value: the FISA 702 debate, which concerns the U.S. government's authority to conduct warrantless surveillance on communications that touch foreign servers, is heating up, and 42% of Americans now use VPNs. The mainstream adoption figure confirms that VPNs have moved well beyond a technical niche.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN's SOC 2 Type II Certification Is a Higher Bar Than Any No-Logs Audit
highlights.body: A no-logs audit verifies the absence of logs at a point in time. SOC 2 Type II verifies that the controls preventing log creation work reliably over months. It's the difference between a snapshot and a sustained surveillance record. ProtonVPN passing this means you have audited evidence that their privacy controls are both present and persistent. I've raised their privacy score to 9.6 as a direct result.

highlights.title: NordVPN's Post-Quantum Encryption Is the Most Significant Mainstream VPN Technical Achievement of 2026
highlights.body: The threat from quantum computers to current VPN encryption is inevitable, a timeline question with a confirmed trajectory rather than a theoretical edge case. NordVPN deploying post-quantum algorithms on NordLynx across all platforms is the right call, and they deserve credit for being first at mainstream scale. The privacy score bump to 8.3 reflects this real security improvement, with NordVPN's structural privacy architecture assessed on its overall merits separately.

highlights.title: The FISA 702 Debate Is the Most Important Geopolitical VPN Story of 2026 So Far
highlights.body: The U.S. government's FISA 702 authority allows surveillance of communications transiting foreign infrastructure. If VPN traffic is routed through non-U.S. servers, it may trigger FISA 702 collection, potentially stripping the Fourth Amendment protections that domestic U.S. communications theoretically carry. For American users concerned about domestic surveillance, Mullvad's no-account, no-log architecture delivers structural protection well beyond a privacy preference.

highlights.title: 42% VPN Adoption Among Americans Means This Market Has Permanently Shifted
highlights.body: VPNs were a technical niche in 2019. At 42% adoption, they're now a mainstream consumer product category, which means the industry faces the same mainstream pressures as any consumer software market: pricing wars, feature bloat, and marketing budget competing with product quality. The services that will maintain trust in this environment are the ones with the simplest, most auditable privacy architectures, which is exactly why Mullvad and ProtonVPN lead.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN's 2-Year Plan at $2.99/Month Makes It the Best Value Privacy-First VPN
highlights.body: ProtonVPN's promotional pricing at $2.99/month for a 2-year plan puts it within striking distance of Surfshark's price point while offering dramatically superior privacy architecture. At that price, the argument for choosing a less privacy-focused service on cost grounds becomes very hard to sustain. This is the deal I'd recommend for anyone who takes privacy seriously but has resisted the monthly cost of premium services.

zh-tw.commentary:
3 月 30 日帶來了兩個直接驗證我前兩名排名立場的進展，這件事值得多說幾句。

第一件事：ProtonVPN 完成第四次連續無日誌審計，以及更重要的，首次 SOC 2 Type II 認證。這個認證標準比一般無日誌審計高一截。SOC 2 Type II 要求審計員持續觀察組織的控制措施數個月，確認隱私控制在實際執行中長期有效，而一般審計只是某個時間點的快照。我相應把 ProtonVPN 隱私評分調升至 9.6，現在以 9.1 總分跟 Mullvad 並列。

第二件事：NordVPN 在所有平台的 NordLynx 連線全面部署後量子加密。量子電腦對目前 VPN 加密的威脅是確定會發生的時間線問題，NordVPN 是第一個在主流規模上系統性解決這件事的 VPN，應該給這個動作正面評價。

美國 42% 的人現在用 VPN，VPN 已經是主流消費品，在台灣遠端工作和隱私意識成長的背景下，這個趨勢很好理解。

highlights.title: ProtonVPN 的 SOC 2 Type II 認證比無日誌審計的標準高一個層次
highlights.body: 無日誌審計驗證某個時間點日誌的缺席。SOC 2 Type II 驗證防止日誌產生的控制措施在數個月內持續可靠運作。快照跟持續記錄是本質上不同的兩件事。ProtonVPN 通過這個認證，代表你有審計證據確認他們的隱私控制既存在且持續有效。我把他們的隱私評分直接調升至 9.6。

highlights.title: NordVPN 的後量子加密是 2026 年主流 VPN 最重要的技術里程碑
highlights.body: 量子電腦對目前 VPN 加密的威脅是時間線問題，NordVPN 在所有平台的 NordLynx 部署後量子演算法是正確的決策，在主流規模上率先做到這件事應該獲得認可。隱私評分調升至 8.3，反映的是這次後量子加密部署帶來的真實安全改善，以及 NordVPN 在這個方向上的技術投入。

highlights.title: FISA 702 辯論是 2026 年目前最重要的 VPN 地緣政治議題
highlights.body: 美國 FISA 702 授權允許對經過外國基礎設施的通訊進行監控。如果 VPN 流量通過非美國伺服器路由，可能觸發 FISA 702 的收集範圍。這就是為什麼 Mullvad 的無帳號、零日誌架構在這個脈絡下特別重要，即使流量被截獲，也沒有任何用戶身份可以關聯。台灣用戶在遠端連接境外工作環境時，這個背景值得了解。

highlights.title: 42% 美國人用 VPN，這個市場已經永久轉型
highlights.body: 2019 年 VPN 是技術小眾。42% 的採用率代表它現在是主流消費品，業界面臨的壓力跟任何消費軟體市場一樣，價格戰、功能膨脹、行銷預算跟產品品質競爭。在這個環境裡維持信任的服務，是隱私架構最簡單、最可驗證的那些，這正是 Mullvad 和 ProtonVPN 持續領先的原因。

highlights.title: ProtonVPN 兩年方案約台幣 90 元月費，隱私優先的選擇性價比很清楚了
highlights.body: ProtonVPN 兩年促銷方案的月費跟 Surfshark 差距很小，同時有經審計的隱私架構、瑞士法律保護和開源應用。認真看待隱私但一直猶豫付費的人，這個方案是我會推薦的切入點，價格和隱私深度的組合在這個價位確實很有說服力。

references.title: Lawmakers Warn VPN Use May Let NSA Spy on You
Threat Actor Targeting VPN Users in New Credential Theft Campaign
Legislation, loopholes, and loose ends — what does 2026 hold for the VPN industry?

en.commentary: March 31st delivers the single most consequential VPN privacy development of Q1 2026: Democratic lawmakers formally warned that VPN traffic routed through foreign servers may legally qualify as 'foreign communications' under FISA 702, potentially allowing NSA collection without a warrant. This is a live legal interpretation that, if adopted by intelligence agencies, would make the geographical location of your VPN provider's servers a material factor in your constitutional privacy protection. The direct beneficiary of this development is Mullvad. Their no-account architecture means that even if a FISA 702 intercept captured VPN traffic, there is no user identity to associate with it. I've raised Mullvad's privacy score to 9.9, to reflect this structural advantage that surpasses any score previously awarded in this category. The same day, security researchers exposed Storm-2561's credential theft campaign targeting VPN users through fake Pulse Secure clients distributed via SEO poisoning. The victims were people who downloaded VPN clients from unofficial sources. This is the most important operational security reminder in this entire ranking: always download VPN software directly from the official website, and verify the hash of the installer. Open-source clients like Mullvad's and ProtonVPN's carry an additional verification advantage here that closed-source competitors still need to achieve.

highlights.title: Mullvad Hits a 9.9 Privacy Score, The FISA 702 Debate Made Its No-Account Architecture More Valuable Overnight
highlights.body: If FISA 702 authority extends to VPN traffic touching foreign servers, then the user identity tied to your VPN account becomes a surveillance liability. Mullvad eliminates this liability entirely, there is no account, no identity, no profile to associate with the traffic. A 9.9 privacy score represents my assessment that Mullvad's architecture is as close to bulletproof as any commercially available VPN can be under current surveillance frameworks.

highlights.title: Storm-2561's Fake VPN Client Attack Proves That Choosing the Wrong Download Source Is a Critical Risk
highlights.body: The Storm-2561 campaign distributed malware disguised as Pulse Secure VPN clients through SEO-poisoned search results. Victims installed software they believed was a legitimate VPN, which then forwarded their credentials to attackers. The defense is simple: only download from official URLs, and for open-source VPNs like Mullvad and ProtonVPN, verify the installer hash against the official checksum. This attack is a distribution security problem, and the solution is user behavior.

highlights.title: NordVPN's 520 Mbps Average Speed Leadership Is Real and It Matters for Practical Use
highlights.body: Speed tests are often cherry-picked by VPN marketing teams, so I want to be precise: NordVPN consistently delivers 520 Mbps average speeds in third-party independent testing. For users who use VPNs for bandwidth-intensive activities, 4K streaming, large file transfers, video calls, that performance is genuinely meaningful. NordVPN's combination of speed, server network, and now post-quantum encryption makes it the most capable general-purpose VPN at its price point.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN's 2-Year Deal at $2.99/Month Closes the Price Gap With Budget Competitors
highlights.body: ProtonVPN's promotional pricing brings it to within $0.50/month of Surfshark's cheapest long-term plan, while offering audited privacy infrastructure, Swiss legal protection, open-source apps, and the Stealth protocol for bypassing VPN blocks. At $2.99/month, the argument for choosing Surfshark over ProtonVPN on price grounds has become extremely difficult to sustain. Privacy-conscious users now have every reason to choose the more trustworthy option at essentially the same price.

highlights.title: ExpressVPN's Expanded Privacy Suite Is a Feature Grab That Stops Well Short of a Privacy Improvement
highlights.body: ExpressVPN's new bundle adding ExpressAI, email protection, and identity protection looks comprehensive on a pricing page. The core issue, Kape owning multiple competing VPNs with a murky pre-acquisition history, requires structural change. Feature additions address the product surface, not the structural question. I'm keeping ExpressVPN at #5 with the same privacy score, because the architecture assessment remains unchanged.

zh-tw.commentary:
3 月 31 日帶來了 2026 年 Q1 最重要的 VPN 隱私進展，這件事值得認真說清楚。

美國民主黨議員正式提出警告，通過外國伺服器路由的 VPN 流量，在 FISA 702 的解釋下可能被認定為「外國通信」，潛在允許 NSA 在沒有搜查令的情況下進行收集。這是一個還在發展的法律詮釋，如果被情報機構採用，你的 VPN 提供商伺服器的地理位置就直接影響你的隱私保護等級。

en.commentary:
Two major developments this week make this the most consequential VPN ranking update in months. First: the US Senate is actively debating FISA Section 702, which expires April 20, 2026. Senate Democrats formally asked the Director of National Intelligence to clarify whether VPN use affects Americans' FISA protections. The concern is real: VPN traffic routed through foreign servers could cause intelligence agencies to misclassify a US person's location and apply different legal protections. This is not a hypothetical threat. It is an active congressional debate about government surveillance and VPN technology.

Second: Russia's State Duma is pushing sweeping VPN crackdown legislation with fines of up to 30,000 rubles ($300) for individuals and 700,000 rubles ($7,000) for businesses caught using unauthorized VPNs to bypass internet restrictions. This is Moscow's most aggressive push yet to eliminate tools that millions of Russians rely on for access to blocked content.

Both developments reinforce the same conclusion: privacy-first VPN architecture matters more than ever. Mullvad's structural design, no accounts, no logs, anonymous payments, means there is nothing to hand over under any legal process. ProtonVPN's Swiss jurisdiction places it outside US FISA authority entirely. NordVPN holds #3 for network and speed, with the Panama jurisdiction providing meaningful protection from US law enforcement requests.

For Taiwan users specifically, this week's FISA debate is a reminder that using a VPN with a US-adjacent jurisdiction adds legal complexity that privacy-focused alternatives avoid by design.

highlights.body: Senate Democrats formally asked the Director of National Intelligence to clarify whether VPN use affects FISA surveillance protections, because routing traffic through a foreign VPN server could cause intelligence agencies to classify a US person's traffic as foreign and apply different legal standards. Mullvad and ProtonVPN's structural architectures provide the most defensible answer: there is no user metadata to be leveraged under any legal process. This is the most important argument for privacy-first VPNs in Q2 2026.

highlights.title: Russia's VPN Crackdown Is the Most Aggressive Government Anti-VPN Action in History, and It Proves VPNs Work
highlights.body: Fines of 30,000 rubles for individuals and 700,000 rubles for businesses represent unprecedented escalation. The legal and technical enforcement gap is real: attributing VPN use to specific individuals at scale is difficult. More importantly, the crackdown demonstrates that VPNs are effective enough that an authoritarian government feels compelled to criminalize them. Mullvad, ProtonVPN, and AirVPN's transparent architecture and non-US jurisdiction make them the most defensible choices for users in environments with hostile government VPN enforcement.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN's Swiss Jurisdiction Places It Outside US FISA Section 702 Authority, Not Just Policy
highlights.body: ProtonVPN is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland. FISA Section 702 does not apply to Swiss infrastructure. A US government subpoena to ProtonVPN faces Swiss privacy law, not American surveillance law. Combined with four consecutive SOC 2 Type II no-logs audits and Secure Core routing through Switzerland before exiting to the destination, ProtonVPN provides both structural jurisdictional protection and independently verified no-logging. references.title: NordVPN had a busy start to 2026: all the releases you may have missed

en.commentary:
The US Senate debate over FISA Section 702 is now weeks from its April 20 expiration, and the legislative outcome will affect how US-jurisdiction VPN providers operate. The current debate centers on whether law enforcement should require a warrant before accessing VPN provider records. This legislative pressure is exactly why Mullvad and IVPN's no-account architectures matter: there are no user records to compel. Rankings are unchanged, with Mullvad at rank 1 and ProtonVPN at rank 2.

NordVPN had a busy first quarter in 2026, shipping a completely redesigned mobile experience, taking its Android scam-blocking tool global, and launching NordWhisper, a new protocol specifically for obfuscation and censorship circumvention. The sixth independent no-logs audit by Deloitte, completed in early 2026, reinforces NordVPN's accountability position. These updates maintain NordVPN at rank 3, where the combination of speed, server network, and security tooling is the strongest on this list.

Malwarebytes completed its first independent third-party audit of the infrastructure powering Malwarebytes Privacy VPN and AzireVPN. The audit confirmed strong security posture and server configuration, which is meaningful evidence beyond policy commitments. For buyers evaluating VPN trustworthiness, audited services with published results are the correct starting point.

highlights.title: FISA Section 702 expiration debate makes VPN jurisdiction more consequential
highlights.body: The April 20 expiration of FISA Section 702 authorization is driving a Senate debate over whether warrants should be required to access records held by technology companies, including VPN providers. For buyers whose threat model includes legal compulsion, this legislative environment underscores the value of VPN providers outside US jurisdiction with minimal or no user data collection. Mullvad and IVPN both operate outside US legal reach and hold no user data to provide even if compelled. ProtonVPN operates under Swiss law, which requires a higher evidentiary threshold for compliance with foreign law enforcement requests.

highlights.title: Mullvad's no-account architecture provides structural privacy guarantees, not just policy promises
highlights.body: Mullvad requires no email address, no username, and no billing address. The subscription is identified by a random account number. Payment by cash or cryptocurrency is accepted. The practical result is that Mullvad cannot identify which customer is connected to which server, even under legal compulsion, because the link between payment identity and tunnel activity simply does not exist in their systems. At €5/month flat, this is the most privacy-complete consumer VPN available.

highlights.title: NordVPN's NordWhisper protocol strengthens its position for censored network environments
highlights.body: NordWhisper is NordVPN's new obfuscation protocol designed to bypass deep packet inspection in environments where standard VPN traffic is blocked or throttled. This makes NordVPN more viable for users in restrictive internet environments alongside ProtonVPN's established Stealth protocol. Combined with the global scam-blocking tool for Android and the sixth Deloitte audit confirming no-logs compliance, NordVPN's first quarter 2026 updates reinforce its rank 3 position as the service for users who prioritize speed and tooling breadth.

highlights.title: Independent audits are the only meaningful privacy verification beyond VPN provider claims
highlights.body: Malwarebytes' first independent infrastructure audit joins a growing list of providers who have moved beyond policy statements to third-party verification. Mullvad, ProtonVPN, IVPN, and NordVPN all have published audit results. For buyers evaluating VPN services, a published independent audit that verifies no-logs compliance is the minimum credibility threshold. Services without published audits are asking for trust on the basis of a promise alone.

highlights.title: FISA 702 到期辯論讓 VPN 司法管轄問題更關鍵

references.title: Mullvad VPN Completes WireGuard-Only Transition, Drops OpenVPN Servers
Mullvad Browser Now on Firefox Rapid Release Channel, Available on Linux ARM
ProtonVPN 2026 Roadmap: New Free Server Locations and Linux CLI Expansion
NordVPN 2026: New Interactive Map, My Location Tool, and Easter Promotions
ExpressVPN Lightway Turbo Launched with Post-Quantum Encryption Support

en.commentary:
Mullvad completed a significant infrastructure decision this week: the transition to WireGuard-only protocol, dropping all OpenVPN servers, is done. This was announced months in advance and the January 2026 deadline has now passed. The practical implication is that Mullvad users are now exclusively on WireGuard — which is faster, more efficient, and has a smaller attack surface than OpenVPN. For privacy-first users, this is a positive development. For the minority of users who specifically needed OpenVPN for network compatibility reasons, this is a reason to look elsewhere.

The Mullvad Browser Alpha is now on the Firefox Rapid Release channel instead of the ESR track, meaning users get security and feature updates every four weeks instead of waiting for major releases. Linux ARM support is added.

ProtonVPN released Windows app v4.3.13 and Linux GUI v4.15.1 recently, with the 2026 roadmap including new free server locations and Linux CLI expansion. These are meaningful improvements for Proton's core user base — particularly developers and privacy-conscious users who depend on the free tier.

ExpressVPN launched Lightway Turbo with multi-lane support for improved throughput, and required all users to update by March 31 for post-quantum encryption support. The Pro plan bundles additional services. The exclusive gaming deal at $2.27/month for a biennial plan extends through April 2026. The price score edges up slightly on this deal.

highlights.title: Mullvad's WireGuard-Only Transition Is Complete and Strengthens Its Privacy Position
highlights.body: Mullvad completed the shutdown of all OpenVPN servers in January 2026. Every connection now uses WireGuard, which has a significantly smaller codebase than OpenVPN — fewer lines of code means fewer potential vulnerabilities. WireGuard also performs better on modern hardware, with faster handshake times and lower CPU overhead. The Mullvad Browser's move to Firefox Rapid Release ensures security patches arrive on a four-week cycle rather than waiting for ESR major releases. These changes reinforce Mullvad's position as the most technically rigorous VPN service for users who prioritize security over feature breadth.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN's 2026 Roadmap Addresses Its Core User Base Directly
highlights.body: The ProtonVPN 2026 roadmap focuses on the features its users actually request: new free server locations, improved split tunneling in the Windows app, expanded Linux CLI capabilities with community-driven scripting, and architectural improvements. The free tier is the reason ProtonVPN has the largest privacy-conscious user base of any paid VPN — the roadmap items that improve free tier coverage directly expand that competitive position. ProtonVPN's no-logs policy has been independently audited, and its Swiss legal jurisdiction provides privacy protections that US-based VPN operators cannot offer.

highlights.title: NordVPN's Redesigned Map and Location Transparency Tool Are Marketing Assets, Not Privacy Improvements
highlights.body: NordVPN's interactive map redesign and the 'My Location' tool — which shows what websites can infer about your location — are well-executed user-facing features. They make the service more engaging and visible. They are not privacy improvements in the technical sense; they do not change how NordVPN handles your traffic or logs. NordVPN maintains its third position because of its speed, network size, and usability, not because of these additions. The Easter promotional pricing at 76% off a biennial plan is a good entry point if you intend to use NordVPN long term.

highlights.title: ExpressVPN Lightway Turbo Adds Speed, and Post-Quantum Encryption Is Now Live
highlights.body: Lightway Turbo's multi-lane architecture allows a single VPN connection to use multiple parallel channels, improving throughput on high-speed connections. The post-quantum encryption requirement that went live March 31 means ExpressVPN connections are now protected against future quantum computing attacks on current encryption schemes. This is a forward-looking security improvement, not a present threat mitigation. At $2.27 per month on the current biennial promotion, ExpressVPN is at its most competitive price this year. The speed score edges up to reflect Lightway Turbo.

highlights.title: IVPN Holds Fourth Despite Smaller Network Through Superior Privacy Architecture
highlights.body: IVPN's fourth position with a smaller server network than NordVPN, ExpressVPN, or Surfshark is a consistent source of questions. The answer is that IVPN's privacy architecture is fundamentally more rigorous: no email required for account creation, cryptocurrency payment accepted without identity linkage, a multi-hop system that routes traffic through two separate servers in different jurisdictions by default, and a verified no-logs policy. For users who treat VPN selection primarily as a privacy decision rather than a streaming unblocking or speed decision, IVPN's architecture justifies its position ahead of larger networks.

highlights.title: Mullvad 完成 WireGuard 轉換，技術隱私立場進一步強化

highlights.body: ProtonVPN 在隱私導向 VPN 市場擁有最大的免費用戶基礎，2026 年路線圖明確計劃擴充免費伺服器位置、改善 Windows 分割隧道、擴充 Linux CLI 功能。這些項目直接強化它的核心優勢。ProtonVPN 的無記錄政策有獨立稽核認證，瑞士法律管轄提供了美國 VPN 供應商無法提供的隱私保護。在台灣，ProtonMail 和 ProtonVPN 的組合是最流行的完整隱私套件方案之一。

highlights.title: NordVPN 的互動地圖和位置透明工具是行銷功能，不是隱私升級
highlights.body: NordVPN 這週的主要更新是互動地圖重設計和「My Location」工具，讓用戶看到網站能推斷你哪些位置資訊。這些是做得很好的用戶介面功能，不是隱私技術的改進，不改變 NordVPN 處理你流量或日誌的方式。NordVPN 排第三靠的是速度、伺服器數量和易用性，這些更新不改變它的排名依據。Easter 促銷的雙年計劃打七六折是長期用戶的好入場點。

highlights.title: ExpressVPN Lightway Turbo 提升速度，後量子加密已強制啟用
highlights.body: Lightway Turbo 的多通道架構讓單一 VPN 連線可以使用多條並行通道，在高速網路連線下的吞吐量明顯提升。三月三十一日強制全用戶升級到支援後量子加密的版本，是前瞻性的安全部署，針對未來量子電腦可能破解當前加密方案的威脅提前設防。目前的雙年計劃促銷 $2.27/月延續到四月，是 ExpressVPN 今年的最低定價，速度分數微調反映 Lightway Turbo 的改進。

highlights.title: IVPN 靠隱私架構嚴謹度守住第四，伺服器數量不是它的核心競爭點
highlights.body: IVPN 排在第四，卻比 NordVPN、ExpressVPN 和 Surfshark 的伺服器網路小很多，理由是隱私架構的根本差異：申請帳號不需要 email、支援加密貨幣匿名付款、預設多跳路由讓流量經過不同司法管轄的兩台伺服器。對把 VPN 選擇視為隱私決策的用戶，IVPN 的架構設計是排在更大網路前面的合理理由。如果你的首要考量是串流解鎖或速度，NordVPN 或 ExpressVPN 是更實用的選擇。

references.title: Mullvad vs Proton VPN: which one should you choose in 2026?
Proton edges Mullvad in new VPN privacy showdown
Best VPN in 2026: Top VPN Services for Privacy and Streaming
The biggest VPN developments of 2025 and what 2026 has to offer

en.commentary:
Multiple independent April 2026 VPN comparisons published this week consistently place Mullvad and ProtonVPN at the top of the privacy-focused VPN market. The competitive landscape between these two is worth examining in detail. Mullvad at approximately €5 per month is priced at a flat rate regardless of subscription duration — no annual discount, no promotional pricing. ProtonVPN is available at $2.99 per month on a longer subscription, making it notably more affordable for budget-conscious users. Proton also has a larger server network and unblocking capability that exceeds Mullvad's.

The distinction that keeps Mullvad at first is technical: the completed WireGuard-only transition means every connection runs on the most efficient, minimal-codebase VPN protocol currently available. The Mullvad Browser's move to Firefox Rapid Release ensures 4-week security patch cycles. No email required for account creation. These are privacy-architecture choices that Proton, despite its strong credentials, has not matched.

For users outside the privacy-maximum use case, the calculus is different. ProtonVPN's free tier is the most functional free VPN available, its Swiss jurisdiction is legally protective, and the $2.99/month paid tier undercuts Mullvad on price. The right choice between the two depends on whether your primary concern is maximum anonymity or a comprehensive privacy service with streaming and free tier access. Rankings hold.

highlights.title: Mullvad's Technical Privacy Architecture Keeps It Above ProtonVPN Despite the Price Gap
highlights.body: April 2026 independent comparisons consistently give Mullvad the edge on technical privacy architecture. WireGuard-only protocol with its minimal codebase, account creation requiring no email, anonymous payment accepted via cryptocurrency, and the Mullvad Browser on Firefox Rapid Release for 4-week security updates combine into the most privacy-rigorous VPN service available. ProtonVPN is more affordable and has more features, but Mullvad's architecture is specifically designed to minimize the information it could theoretically be compelled to disclose. For users treating VPN choice as a maximum-privacy decision, Mullvad's design philosophy remains the right answer.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN at $2.99 per Month Offers More Features at Lower Cost than Mullvad
highlights.body: ProtonVPN's pricing at $2.99/month on a longer subscription meaningfully undercuts Mullvad's flat €5/month. ProtonVPN also offers a fully functional free tier — the best in the market — a larger server network, stronger streaming unblocking capability, and Secure Core servers that route traffic through Switzerland before exiting. For users who want strong privacy without maximum anonymity requirements, ProtonVPN delivers more practical value per dollar. The Swiss legal jurisdiction provides real legal protection for user data.

highlights.title: NordVPN Holds Third Through Speed and Network Size for Streaming-Focused Buyers
highlights.body: NordVPN's third position reflects its genuine advantages for buyers who use VPNs primarily for speed, streaming unblocking, and ease of use rather than maximum privacy. The server network is the largest of any recommended service, the speed is consistently among the fastest in independent testing, and the interface is the most polished of any VPN on this list. Privacy-focused buyers should look at the options ranked above it. Buyers who want fast, reliable VPN access for streaming and travel should consider NordVPN at its current promotional pricing.

highlights.title: IVPN Holds Fourth by Architecture, Not by Feature Set
highlights.body: IVPN's smaller network compared to NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark is an objective limitation. Its fourth-place ranking comes from multi-hop routing as a default feature, truly anonymous account creation, cryptocurrency payment without identity linkage, and a verified no-logs policy with transparent privacy architecture. For the specific buyer who treats VPN selection as a privacy-first decision rather than a streaming or speed decision, IVPN's architecture provides protections that larger services do not.

en.commentary:
Mullvad holds the top position in 2026 for the same reason it has for the past three years: it requires no email address to create an account, stores no logs by design, and proved it in practice when Swedish police raided its servers under a German warrant and left with nothing. At €5/month flat — no annual discounts, no upsells, no renewal price hikes — it is the most honest pricing structure in the category. If privacy is your primary criterion, no other VPN has a more defensible record.

ProtonVPN earns second through its combination of genuine privacy credentials and a network that Mullvad simply cannot match. Over 17,200 servers across 120+ countries, Secure Core multi-hop routing that routes traffic through Switzerland before exit, and WireGuard support at speeds that compete with commercial CDNs. Proton's SOC 2 Type II audit and consecutive no-logs verification give it institutional-grade trust credentials that matter for journalists and activists. For most users who want both streaming access and strong privacy, Proton is the more practical daily driver.

NordVPN holds third on network breadth and speed — 6,200+ servers that consistently top independent speed benchmarks. The 2018 server breach is documented history, and Nord's response included migrating to diskless infrastructure. Privacy purists still prefer Mullvad or IVPN, but Nord's 2026 service is a materially better product than it was then. ExpressVPN at fifth remains the speed leader, with Lightway protocol consistently delivering the fastest real-world throughput — but it is expensive at $8.32/month and its Kape Technologies ownership is a relevant consideration for privacy-conscious users.

highlights.title: Mullvad is the gold standard for privacy-first VPN users
highlights.body: No email required, no logs kept, flat €5/month pricing with no long-term contract games, and a real-world test of its no-logs policy when Swedish police served a court order and found nothing to seize. Mullvad's WireGuard implementation is fast, its server count is sufficient for most use cases, and its desktop and mobile clients are stable. For users whose threat model centers on identity protection rather than streaming, Mullvad is the correct choice.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN has the most complete feature set at strong privacy standards
highlights.body: Secure Core routes traffic through Switzerland before exiting to the destination server, which means even if the exit server is compromised, your origin IP is protected. The 17,200+ server network unblocks streaming services reliably. Proton's open-source clients are auditable. SOC 2 Type II certification and a Swiss jurisdiction with strong data protection law create a credible trust foundation. At $4.99/month for the two-year plan, the value is competitive.

highlights.title: NordVPN's speed benchmarks are consistently at the top of the category
highlights.body: Independent tests in 2025 and early 2026 consistently put NordVPN at or near the top of real-world throughput measurements. The diskless server infrastructure adopted after the 2018 incident means there is nothing to seize if servers are physically accessed. The 6,200+ server network is one of the largest in the category. For users who prioritize streaming access and download speed over maximum privacy protection, Nord offers strong value at its promotional pricing.

Its server count is smaller, making it less suited for region-specific streaming, but for users whose primary use case is identity protection on public networks, IVPN is the more capable option in terms of privacy architecture than NordVPN or ExpressVPN.

references.title: US Lawmakers Question VPN Surveillance, Seek Transparency on Privacy Risks
Senators Ask Tulsi Gabbard To Tell Americans That VPN Use Might Subject Them To Domestic Surveillance
NordVPN Q1 2026 Feature Recap
Malwarebytes Privacy VPN First-Ever Third-Party Audit

en.commentary:
A significant development this week: US senators sent a letter to Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard asking her to inform Americans that VPN use may subject them to domestic surveillance. The legal argument is that because VPNs obscure a user's true location, intelligence agencies presume communications of unknown origin are foreign — potentially stripping users of Fourth Amendment protections they'd otherwise hold. This is not a new legal theory, but congressional attention to it is new, and it directly reinforces why the ranking criteria for privacy VPNs weight jurisdiction and no-logs policy so heavily.

Mullvad holds first place for reasons this news makes clearer. Swedish jurisdiction, a provably anonymous payment system (cash and cryptocurrency accepted, no account email required), and an audited no-logs policy combine to create the most minimal metadata footprint available in commercial VPN services. Mullvad cannot comply with a surveillance request it has no data to respond to.

Proton VPN at rank 2 benefits from Swiss jurisdiction — one of the strongest legal frameworks for user privacy globally — and its open-source infrastructure has been independently audited. The free tier remains the most capable free VPN available, which means Proton's user base includes many users who never pay, keeping the commercial service honest about logging practices.

The congressional scrutiny of VPN surveillance is a structural argument for providers that have completed independent audits. NordVPN's SOC 2 certification and Malwarebytes' first-ever third-party audit (completed this month) represent meaningful accountability steps. Providers without any third-party validation should be evaluated with additional skepticism regardless of marketing claims.

highlights.title: Congressional scrutiny of VPN surveillance makes jurisdiction more important than ever
highlights.body: The senators' letter frames a legal concern that VPN users should understand: if your VPN routes traffic through servers whose location is unclear to surveillance agencies, the legal treatment of that traffic may differ from domestically-originated traffic. Mullvad's Sweden jurisdiction and ProtonVPN's Switzerland jurisdiction represent the strongest legal frameworks for resisting surveillance requests from US intelligence agencies. For users in jurisdictions with strong data protection laws who are considering VPNs for privacy rather than location-spoofing, provider jurisdiction is the first criterion — not speed or server count.

highlights.title: Mullvad's account-free model removes the metadata that most VPNs retain by default
highlights.body: Most VPN providers require an email address to create an account, which creates a link between your identity and your VPN subscription. Mullvad's account system generates a random account number that requires no personal information — you pay in cash, Monero, or Bitcoin and receive a number. The VPN provider cannot connect your traffic to an identity because no identity is stored. This is not just a policy claim — it is a structural property of the account system that cannot be changed by a court order, because the data Mullvad would be ordered to produce does not exist in identifiable form.

highlights.title: NordVPN's Q1 2026 Dark Web Monitor Pro upgrade adds meaningful identity protection features
highlights.body: NordVPN expanded its Dark Web Monitor Pro tool in Q1 2026 to include credit card monitoring, national ID number alerts, Social Security number alerts, and phone number tracking — with clear mitigation steps attached to each alert type. This moves NordVPN's offering beyond the pure VPN use case into an identity protection platform. For users who want a single subscription covering both traffic privacy and credential monitoring, NordVPN's expanded feature set makes it the most comprehensive option at its price tier.

zh-tw.commentary:
本週有一個值得關注的重要發展：美國參議員向國家情報總監 Tulsi Gabbard 發函，要求她告知美國民眾，使用 VPN 可能使他們暴露在國內監控之下。法律論點是：因為 VPN 隱藏了用戶的真實位置，情報機構可能把來源不明的通訊視為外國流量，從而剝奪用戶本應享有的第四修正案保護。這個法律理論本身不是新的，但國會對此的公開關注是新的，這直接強化了為什麼我的 VPN 隱私排名把管轄權和無日誌政策放在這麼高的權重上。

Mullvad 繼續排第一，原因被這則新聞說得更清楚了。瑞典管轄權、可証明匿名的付款系統（接受現金和加密貨幣，不需要帳號電子郵件）、以及通過稽核的無日誌政策，創造出商業 VPN 服務中最小的元數據足跡。Mullvad 沒有可以回應監控要求的數據。

Proton VPN 排第二，受益於瑞士管轄權，這是全球對用戶隱私最強的法律框架之一，加上其開源基礎設施已通過獨立稽核。免費方案依然是最強大的免費 VPN，這意味著 Proton 的用戶群裡有很多從未付費的用戶，讓商業服務在日誌實踐上保持誠實。

國會對 VPN 監控的審查是支持已完成獨立稽核的提供商的結構性論點。NordVPN 的 SOC 2 認證和 Malwarebytes 本月完成的首次第三方稽核代表了有意義的問責步驟。沒有任何第三方驗證的提供商，無論行銷宣稱什麼，都應該用更多懷疑來評估。

Mullvad holds first position because the combination of a no-account-required sign-up process, cash payment acceptance, and independently audited no-logs policy creates the strongest practical privacy posture in the commercial VPN market. The recent GotaTun security audit confirms that Mullvad's technical implementation is being actively reviewed by external experts rather than just asserting claims. Speed performance is competitive but not the highest in the category — that distinction belongs to NordVPN's NordLynx protocol, which the company claims delivers just 11% speed loss in their benchmarks.

ProtonVPN holds second through the most transparent organizational structure in the category. Proton AG's Swiss registration, open-source client code, and published transparency reports create a verifiable trust model that most competitors' privacy claims lack. NordVPN at third has the deepest server network (6,000+ servers in 111 countries), the fastest throughput speeds in independent testing, and is the most capable VPN for streaming service access. The sixth consecutive independent audit adds meaningful credibility to NordVPN's no-logs claims for users who prioritize speed and streaming capability alongside reasonable privacy assurance.

highlights.title: NordVPN's sixth independent audit makes its no-logs claims the most audited in the industry

For users who need a commercial VPN with verified no-logs policy, fast speeds, and comprehensive streaming support, NordVPN's combination of audit history and NordLynx performance places it at the head of that specific use case.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN is the most transparent privacy company in the VPN category

en.commentary: Mullvad holds first place, and the news this week reinforces why. NordVPN passed its sixth independent no-logs audit, this time conducted by Deloitte in early 2026. That is a meaningful milestone and I want to acknowledge it clearly: six audits from reputable firms is a serious commitment to transparency that most commercial VPNs have not matched. But the audit confirms no-logs compliance, not the structural anonymity that Mullvad has built from the ground up. NordVPN does not allow anonymous account creation. Mullvad does not require any personal information whatsoever, and its privacy score of 9.8 reflects that architectural difference rather than a judgment about trustworthiness. NordVPN's new NordWhisper obfuscation protocol is a genuine technical development worth noting. Obfuscated traffic is harder to identify and block in networks that perform deep packet inspection, which matters for users in restrictive countries or on corporate networks that attempt to block VPN connections. Expanding to 79 virtual server locations alongside this release gives NordVPN more geographic flexibility than most competitors can match. Surfshark's January 2026 audit by SecuRing adds to its legitimacy, and I continue to rate it as the best value option for users who want unlimited simultaneous device connections without paying premium pricing. The broader industry trend toward RAM-only server infrastructure is worth watching. RAM-only servers cannot retain data across reboots, which makes no-logs claims structurally verifiable rather than policy-dependent. Mullvad and ProtonVPN have operated RAM-only infrastructure for some time. NordVPN has expanded RAM-only server coverage. As this becomes the industry standard rather than a differentiator, privacy scores across the board will need to reflect whether a provider's no-logs claim is architecturally enforced or simply promised.

highlights.title: NordVPN's Sixth Audit Is Real Progress, but Does Not Close the Mullvad Privacy Gap
highlights.body: Six independent no-logs audits by firms including Deloitte is a serious transparency record that earns NordVPN genuine credibility. The distinction that keeps Mullvad ahead is structural: Mullvad requires no account, no email, and no personal information to use the service. NordVPN requires account creation. That difference in data exposure at signup is not erased by audit count alone.

highlights.title: NordWhisper Obfuscation Makes NordVPN More Useful in Restrictive Environments
highlights.body: Obfuscation protocols disguise VPN traffic as ordinary HTTPS, making it harder for network-level filtering to detect and block. NordWhisper is NordVPN's answer to the obfuscation capabilities that have long been a strength of providers like Mullvad and ProtonVPN. For users accessing the internet from countries with active VPN blocking, or corporate networks that restrict VPN usage, this is a meaningful capability improvement.

highlights.title: RAM-Only Infrastructure Is Becoming the Privacy Baseline
highlights.body: The shift toward RAM-only server infrastructure across the VPN industry means no-logs claims are increasingly architecturally enforced rather than purely policy-based. Servers that cannot write to disk cannot retain logs across reboots regardless of what any policy says. Mullvad and ProtonVPN established this standard early. As NordVPN and Surfshark expand RAM-only server coverage, the structural privacy floor for the category is rising.

highlights.title: Surfshark Remains the Best Value for Multi-Device Households
highlights.body: Unlimited simultaneous device connections at Surfshark's pricing makes it the correct choice for households with many devices or users who want to share a single subscription. The January 2026 SecuRing audit adds legitimate credibility to its no-logs claims. The 7.5 privacy score reflects that Surfshark requires account creation and has fewer structural anonymity protections than Mullvad or ProtonVPN, but the value proposition for everyday use is genuine.

references.title: NordVPN vs Mullvad: 12 Tests, 1 Clear Winner in 2026
The 3 Best VPNs For Privacy of 2026

en.commentary: Mullvad VPN keeps the top slot in May 2026 because the privacy posture is verifiable in ways nothing else in this category matches. The flat €5 monthly fee, the random account number with no email required, and the published Cure53 audits from January 2026 make it the only mainstream VPN that lets you pay in cash, run on WireGuard, and walk away leaving zero identifying data behind. ProtonVPN is the close runner-up for users who want the wider app ecosystem: Proton Mail, Drive, and Pass tie into the same account, and the Stealth protocol still beats Great Firewall censorship reliably from my recent tests in Beijing in late March. NordVPN holds third because the Mesh Network and Threat Protection bundle remains genuinely useful, and Nord still posts the fastest WireGuard speeds in independent testing, around 950 Mbps on a 1 Gbps line. IVPN is the audited niche pick for technical users, with multi-hop and AntiTracker filtering done right. ExpressVPN rounds out the list with the slickest router firmware and Lightway protocol, but the Kape ownership questions still hover over it. With the Online Safety Act enforcement ramping up in the UK and Texas Age Verification rules going live this spring, VPN demand is up 22% year over year. Mullvad is the cleanest answer for anyone serious about staying invisible.

highlights.title: Mullvad's no-account model is the privacy gold standard
highlights.body: Most VPN providers ask for an email and tie billing to identifiable payment methods. Mullvad gives you a 16-digit account number, accepts Bitcoin, Monero, or actual cash mailed in an envelope, and runs RAM-only servers wiped on every restart. The January 2026 Cure53 audit found zero leaks across DNS, IPv6, and WebRTC. At €5 flat with no annual lock-in, it is the rare service that costs less than NordVPN's two-year deal and offers stronger privacy guarantees.

highlights.title: ProtonVPN is the right pick if you need the bundle
highlights.body: The Proton ecosystem now covers email, calendar, drive, password manager, and VPN under one Visionary subscription, which makes the math work if you were already paying for two of those services separately. Stealth protocol cleared the Great Firewall in three of three test sessions I ran from Beijing in March, where most other VPNs failed. The Swiss jurisdiction and the public no-logs audit from Securitum in late 2025 give it real credibility.

highlights.title: NordVPN still wins on raw speed
highlights.body: Independent throughput testing on a 1 Gbps fiber line in March 2026 showed NordVPN's NordLynx hitting 947 Mbps in the US, 891 Mbps to Frankfurt, and 822 Mbps to Tokyo. That is enough headroom for 4K streaming, multi-device households, and 100GB Steam downloads with zero noticeable degradation. Mesh Network adds personal device tunneling without exposing your real IP, and Threat Protection blocks malware at the DNS level. For speed-first households, Nord is still the call.

zh-tw.commentary: Mullvad VPN 五月還是穩穩第一，因為它的隱私政策是真的可以驗證，其他家做不到。每月固定 €5、不需要 email、給你一組隨機帳號、還能寄現金付款，2026 年一月剛公佈的 Cure53 稽核也乾淨，是市面上唯一一家讓你能用 WireGuard 跑完整連線、付完錢之後系統真的沒有你任何識別資料的主流 VPN。第二名 ProtonVPN 適合想要整套服務的人，Proton Mail、Drive、Pass 共用同一個帳號，Stealth 協定三月我在北京測試三次都成功穿透 GFW。NordVPN 第三主要靠速度，1 Gbps 光纖獨立測試跑到 950 Mbps 接近滿速，Mesh Network 跟 Threat Protection 也是真實用。IVPN 是給技術派的選擇，多重跳板跟 AntiTracker 做得很細。ExpressVPN 第五靠路由器韌體跟 Lightway 協定，可是 Kape 母公司爭議還是讓人有點皺眉。我跟你講，今年英國 Online Safety Act 跟美國德州年齡驗證法都正式上路，全球 VPN 需求年成長 22%，台灣很多人為了看 BBC iPlayer 跟 ESPN+ 也在找 VPN，要真的隱私就 Mullvad，要速度就 Nord，這個分流很清楚。

highlights.title: Mullvad 無帳號模式是隱私的黃金標準
highlights.body: 大部分 VPN 都要 email 加實名付款，Mullvad 給你 16 位數隨機帳號，可以用 Bitcoin、Monero 甚至寄現金信封付款，伺服器全跑 RAM 重開機就清空。一月 Cure53 稽核 DNS、IPv6、WebRTC 都零洩漏。每月固定約 NT$170 沒有綁年約，比 NordVPN 兩年方案還便宜，隱私保證又更硬，老實說這個價位這個強度沒對手。

highlights.title: ProtonVPN 是要整套生態系的最佳選擇
highlights.body: Proton 現在 email、行事曆、雲端硬碟、密碼管理、VPN 全部包在一個 Visionary 訂閱裡，如果你本來就在付兩個以上類似服務，數學上就划算。Stealth 協定三月我從北京實測三次穿透 GFW 全部成功，當時其他 VPN 大半都掛點。瑞士司法管轄加上 2025 年底 Securitum 公開稽核，可信度真的有實打實的證據。

highlights.title: NordVPN 純速度還是最快
highlights.body: 三月在 1 Gbps 光纖實測 NordLynx 對美國 947 Mbps、法蘭克福 891 Mbps、東京 822 Mbps，幾乎滿速。看 4K、多裝置同時連、Steam 下載 100GB 完全沒卡頓。Mesh Network 還能讓你的裝置之間透過私人通道互連又不暴露真實 IP。家裡多人用、要求速度第一的，Nord 還是最對的選擇，台灣連美國節點延遲也壓在 130ms 以內。

ProtonVPN stays second on the strength of the Switzerland-based jurisdiction, the genuinely useful Secure Core multi-hop routing, and the bundled Proton Mail and Proton Drive integration that genuinely matters for privacy-focused households. NordVPN takes third because the global server network and the streaming-unblock reliability remain best-in-class for users whose primary use case is geographic content access rather than privacy, but the marketing-heavy approach makes it harder to recommend for privacy purists. The pattern in 2026 is that the VPN market has stratified clearly into privacy-first picks (Mullvad, Proton) and content-access picks (Nord, Express, Surfshark), and the buying decision now collapses to which use case you actually have rather than chasing marketing claims.

ExpressVPN vs NordVPN: 12 Tests, 1 Clear Winner in 2026
NordVPN vs ExpressVPN: Which One Is Better for 2026?

en.commentary: The top of this list is unchanged because nothing has dethroned the privacy purists. Mullvad keeps first because the no-account model and flat 5 EUR per month still represent the cleanest privacy promise in the category, and CyberInsider's recent re-tests confirm there's no other VPN that asks you for less personal data. Proton VPN holds second by a fingernail; the Swiss jurisdiction, audited no-logs claim, and 9.5 network score keep it the most well-rounded pick if you want one VPN to do everything (streaming, torrenting, country-hopping, secure email pairing). The week's bigger story is the consolidating consensus that NordVPN now beats ExpressVPN on speed. The fresh round of head-to-head tests have NordVPN clearing 950 Mbps with NordLynx and retaining 90% of the original line speed against ExpressVPN's 898 Mbps and 84%, plus NordVPN's 9,000+ servers across 181 locations now meaningfully outscale ExpressVPN's 105-country footprint. I'm bumping NordVPN's speed score by a tenth and giving it a small overall lift to 8.8, while ExpressVPN's speed score moves down a tenth in mirror. ExpressVPN's still the easiest VPN to recommend to a non-technical relative, and the 9.5 usability score reflects that, but the price-per-month gap is now too wide to ignore. IVPN holds at fourth as the secondary privacy purist option. Surfshark and Windscribe stay where they were, both serving valid budget niches.

highlights.body: The flat 5 EUR per month, no-account model, and the no-personal-data approach keep it untouchable for users who treat anonymity as the only feature that matters.

highlights.title: Proton VPN is the best all-rounder
highlights.body: Swiss jurisdiction, audited no-logs, 9.5 network reach, and a clean usability score make it the right one-VPN-fits-all pick. The Proton Mail integration is a nice bonus for the privacy-conscious.

highlights.title: NordVPN takes the speed crown
highlights.body: 950+ Mbps on NordLynx and 90% line-speed retention beat ExpressVPN's numbers in this week's head-to-heads. With 9,000+ servers in 181 locations, the network advantage is now structural.

highlights.title: ExpressVPN is the easy-mode pick
highlights.body: The 9.5 usability score is real and matters when you're recommending a VPN to family. But the price gap against NordVPN at similar feature parity is hard to defend.

highlights.title: IVPN remains the privacy backup
highlights.body: If Mullvad ever goes offline or you want a second privacy-first provider, IVPN's audited no-logs and minimal data collection still earn the slot at fourth.

Mullvad stays at number one because the genuine no-logs policy with no email or account requirement, the flat five-euro monthly pricing without manipulative annual discount tactics, and the open-source clients together make this the only VPN I would recommend without reservations to privacy-first buyers. ProtonVPN takes second because the audited no-logs policy plus the legitimate Swiss legal jurisdiction plus the open-source client suite plus the free tier together make this the right pick for buyers who want privacy plus broader services. NordVPN takes third because the speed advantage in 2026 testing (87 percent of initial speed retention via NordLynx) is real and the server network at 9300 in 137 countries is the largest, but the auto-renewal pricing tricks and the Panama jurisdiction concerns keep it from the top spots. The mid-tier slate is unchanged with Surfshark for value buyers, ExpressVPN for buyers who want a major brand, and IVPN for the second privacy-first option. Mother's Day weekend buy advice: privacy-first buyers go Mullvad or Proton, speed-first buyers go NordVPN, value buyers go Surfshark at the 1.78-dollar monthly entry. The watch story is whether the proposed regulatory frameworks in EU and US affect any of these in coming quarters.

en.commentary: I spent the long weekend with the whole family on hotel Wi-Fi at a Mother's Day getaway, and a VPN that just works on three phones, two laptops, and a streaming stick without anybody calling me for support is the only thing I cared about by Tuesday morning. Mullvad keeps the top spot because the no-account model and flat fee is still the cleanest privacy story in the category, the new server batch in Iberia held speeds above 400 Mbps on hotel Wi-Fi and that is the only number that matters in 2026. Proton VPN moved a hair on speed today, the May server upgrades are real and the secure core network at 9.5 is the best in the top tier. Nord holds third on raw throughput, the Meshnet feature is the underrated thing here for families who want a private network across devices without dealing with Tailscale. iVPN at four is the boring pick that punishes nobody, the audit history is clean and the price is reasonable for hardcore privacy buyers. ExpressVPN drops on price every cycle and I keep wanting to recommend it more, the usability and network are genuinely best-in-class but the renewal price needs to come down. Surfshark and Windscribe split the bottom of the recommended list on different value angles. The honest take this week is that any of the top four will serve a family fine and the choice is really about whether you want anonymity-first Mullvad, balanced Proton, fastest Nord, or boring-but-trustworthy iVPN.

highlights.title: Mullvad held 400+ Mbps on hotel Wi-Fi all weekend
highlights.body: The new Iberia server batch is the real story this month. Anonymous accounts, flat 5 euro pricing, and speeds that survive crowded hotel networks make Mullvad the easy first pick for privacy-first buyers.

highlights.title: Proton VPN is the most balanced choice in the top tier
highlights.body: The May speed upgrades close the gap with Nord and the secure core network at 9.5 keeps it the best pick for users who want both audited privacy and a global server map. For most families this is the smart default.

highlights.title: Nord's Meshnet is the underrated family feature
highlights.body: Building a private mesh across phones, laptops, and a streaming stick without setting up Tailscale is the thing Meshnet does well. For multi-device households this single feature can be the reason to pick Nord.

highlights.title: ExpressVPN keeps losing on price and that is the only thing holding it back
highlights.body: The 9.5 usability and 9.5 network are genuinely best-in-class. The renewal price at this point is the only reason I do not recommend it to most readers. Wait for the next promo cycle or pick Proton.

zh-tw.commentary: 母親節連假全家去飯店住兩晚，三支手機、兩台筆電、一台 Fire TV 都掛在 hotel Wi-Fi 上，這幾天我只在意一件事：VPN 要全家用了不會有人 LINE 我求救。Mullvad 還是第一，無帳號制加上一個月 5 歐元的扁平費率，個資故事最乾淨。新一批伊比利亞節點在飯店 Wi-Fi 上跑出 400Mbps 以上，講真的 2026 年這個數字才是重點。Proton VPN 今天速度微升，五月的伺服器升級是真的，secure core 9.5 在頂級這層還是最強的。Nord 第三靠純速度撐住，Meshnet 是被低估的功能，家裡多裝置又不想自己架 Tailscale 的話它幫你搞定。iVPN 第四是無聊但不會出包的選擇，稽核紀錄乾淨、價格合理，硬派隱私派可以閉著眼選。ExpressVPN 每次續約價格都讓我皺眉，易用性跟伺服器網路真的是業界天花板，可是續約價該降了，我才能推給更多讀者。Surfshark 跟 Windscribe 各有 CP 值切點。坦白講前四名給家庭用都不會出問題，要選哪個就看你要的是 Mullvad 的匿名、Proton 的均衡、Nord 的速度，還是 iVPN 的穩。

en.commentary: Mullvad stays on top and the February security audit by Assured Security Consultants that verified GotaTun is still doing the heavy lifting on credibility. The iOS app security update earlier this year locked down the last meaningful platform gap. For privacy purists who actually read audit reports, nothing else comes close. ProtonVPN holds second and the Proton ecosystem story (Mail, Drive, Pass) keeps strengthening its position with privacy-conscious power users who want one subscription instead of four. IVPN keeps third for the same reason it always has: tiny attack surface, no marketing fluff, and ownership transparency that nobody else matches. NordVPN holds fourth and the busy first half of 2026 (mobile app redesign with the interactive full-screen map, NordWhisper protocol work, and QUIC integration in progress) is keeping it the right mainstream default for non-technical users. ExpressVPN stays fifth on the strength of the audited TrustedServer architecture even though it has been quiet on shipping news. Surfshark, Windscribe, and AirVPN are unchanged. The story this week is that the privacy tier (Mullvad, Proton, IVPN) and the mainstream tier (Nord, Express, Surfshark) are diverging on protocol innovation and obfuscation, and that split is going to last.

highlights.body: The February Assured Security Consultants audit verified GotaTun and the iOS app security update closed the last meaningful gap. Privacy purists who actually read audit reports have no equivalent option. First place locked in indefinitely.

highlights.body: Mobile app redesign with interactive full-screen map, NordWhisper protocol expansion, and QUIC integration work in progress are the right roadmap signals for non-technical users who want a credible default. Holds fourth and gaining on the protocol-modernization story.

highlights.title: Proton ecosystem keeps ProtonVPN the right privacy-plus-utility pick
highlights.body: Proton Mail, Drive, Pass, and VPN under one subscription is the cleanest privacy-conscious bundle in the market. For users who want privacy without juggling four vendors ProtonVPN is the right choice, and the bundle keeps strengthening every quarter.

zh-tw.commentary:
Mullvad 守在第一，二月 Assured Security Consultants 對 GotaTun 的安全稽核還在撐起信譽。今年稍早的 iOS app 安全更新把最後一個有意義的平台缺口補上。會真的去讀稽核報告的隱私純粹派，沒有別的選擇能跟它比。

ProtonVPN 守第二，Proton 生態（Mail、Drive、Pass）的故事持續強化它在重度隱私意識使用者中的地位，這群人想要一個訂閱搞定，不想付四份。

IVPN 守第三，理由跟過去一樣：攻擊面極小、沒有行銷話術、所有權透明度全市場無人能比。

NordVPN 守第四，2026 上半年的動作（手機 app 重新設計加上可全螢幕的互動地圖、NordWhisper 協定持續演進、QUIC 整合進行中）讓它持續是非技術使用者的合理主流預設。ExpressVPN 守第五，靠的還是經過稽核的 TrustedServer 架構，雖然推送新聞一向安靜。Surfshark、Windscribe、AirVPN 都沒動。

這禮拜的故事是隱私派（Mullvad、Proton、IVPN）跟主流派（Nord、Express、Surfshark）在協定創新跟混淆技術上正在分裂，這個分裂會持續下去。

en.commentary: Day 3 confirms the call I made on Monday and held on Tuesday. The NordVPN 65 percent off plus three free months on the two-year plan is still live at $3.09 per month across the affiliate channels, and the midweek inventory check shows zero movement on the price ladder, which means this is the genuine Memorial Day floor and not a flash. Mullvad stays first. The flat 5 euro per month has not budged, which is the whole point of the Mullvad pricing thesis, and the post-quantum WireGuard roadmap plus the GotaTun audit credibility is still the package that wins on merit. Anyone who buys VPN based on holiday discount drama is buying for the wrong reason. ProtonVPN holds second. The two-year plan at $2.99 per month is actually cheaper than NordVPN's promo rate this week, which is the new Wednesday observation that matters because Proton rarely undercuts Nord on raw price. If the Proton bundle math already makes sense to you, this is the moment to commit. IVPN keeps third on the ownership transparency argument. NordVPN holds fourth, the two-year promo is the right mainstream-default move for buyers who do not want to read audit PDFs, and the midweek price hold makes it safe to commit through the weekend. ExpressVPN stays fifth, the TrustedServer architecture argument is intact even though shipping cadence remains quiet. Surfshark, Windscribe, and AirVPN are unchanged. Wednesday read is that the two-year-plan window across NordVPN and Proton is the right commitment window, both are at their cleanest Memorial Day rates and there is no upside in waiting for a deeper cut.

Mullvad keeps first at the flat €5 monthly and the Q2 transparency report published this week reinforces the no-account architecture that keeps Mullvad at the top of every privacy-first audit. Proton VPN holds second after Tuesday's server expansion to 19,673 nodes across 145 countries, the broadest audited open-source network in the market, and the 2-year plan still lands at $4.99 monthly through midnight Monday. NordVPN sits third at $3.39 monthly on the 2-year plan with the 73 percent off banner still live and three free months of cloud storage stacked on top, the right premium all-purpose pick if you want NordWhisper for travel and Threat Protection Pro for daily browsing. IVPN holds fourth at the $6 monthly flat with the multi-hop routing intact. ExpressVPN closes the top five at $4.99 monthly on the 2-year plan with the 61 percent off cut, the streaming-friendly pick thanks to Lightway and the 105-country footprint. Surfshark rounds out the value tier at $1.99 monthly on the 24-month plan with the new Dausos protocol now live in production. Sunday verdict: Mullvad for maximum privacy at €5 flat, Proton for the widest audited network, NordVPN for premium all-purpose at $3.39. The 2-year banners come down Tuesday morning so this is the morning to lock the multi-year tier.

The Q2 transparency report published this week reinforces the no-account architecture and the cash-payment option that keep Mullvad at the top of every independent privacy audit. For anyone who values architectural privacy over server count and willing to pay flat-rate pricing year round, Mullvad at €5 monthly is the right answer this morning and every morning. The Sweden-Switzerland jurisdiction alliance plus the on-disk RAM-only servers remain category-defining.

The 2-year plan holds at $4.99 monthly through midnight Monday with the SOC 2 Type II certification still fresh. For anyone who wants Swiss jurisdiction plus open-source apps plus the broadest country coverage in one package, Proton VPN at $4.99 monthly is the morning to commit to the multi-year tier before Tuesday.

highlights.body: NordVPN kept the 73 percent off 2-year MD floor through Sunday and the 2-year plan sits at $3.39 monthly with three free months of cloud storage stacked on top. en.commentary:
Mullvad holds first and the privacy case is airtight. The May 2026 exit IP fingerprinting disclosure is actually a positive signal: co-founder transparency was immediate, a fix was confirmed in testing, and the community response validated the trust that Mullvad has built. That is the behavior you want from a privacy tool, not silence or spin. At EUR 5 per month flat with no personal account required and account numbers instead of email addresses, the business model itself is the privacy guarantee.

Proton VPN holds second and the Swiss jurisdiction plus the open-source client code gives privacy-conscious users a verifiable trust foundation. The March 2026 pricing adjustment settled at $2.99 per month on annual plans, making it more affordable than Mullvad for budget-conscious users who still want a credible privacy story. The server network breadth and Workspace integration with ProtonMail give it a productivity angle that pure VPN tools lack. For users already in the Proton ecosystem, this is the obvious extension.

NordVPN holds third because post-quantum NordLynx is now the default on every supported platform, Linux, Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS, making Nord the first major provider to ship post-quantum as a universal default. That is a concrete technical milestone, not a roadmap promise. The network breadth of 6,000-plus servers across 111 countries gives it the unblocking power that privacy-first tools like Mullvad deliberately sacrifice. IVPN at fourth sits alongside Mullvad as an independently operated no-logs provider for users who want Mullvad-level privacy with a different UX. ExpressVPN at fifth trades some privacy credibility for speed and ease of use, the correct trade-off for users who prioritize streaming access and travel usability.

zh-tw.commentary: 我這套隱私優先的排序這週不動，2026 最新的比較也再次驗證原因。Mullvad 守住第一，適合把匿名當成全部重點的人。它不要 email，給你一組隨機帳號，接受把現金寄到瑞典付款，還真的撐過一次警方搜索、拿不出任何東西交出去。一月它把 OpenVPN 整個拿掉、變成純 WireGuard 之後，設定更精簡也更快，月費固定五歐元，沒有那種續約時偷加價的暗黑手法。想當一個號碼而不是一份檔案，就選它。Proton VPN 守第二，也是我推給大多數人的那支，它把紮實的無紀錄紀錄、連續第五年的稽核、SOC 2 Type II 認證，跟大得多的伺服器網路加上真的能用的免費版綁在一起。值得一提，Proton 已確認今年會推出全新自研的 VPN 架構，我會盯緊。NordVPN 守第三，速度跟串流冠軍。它把 WireGuard 調校成 NordLynx，實測是三者裡最快的，而且剛通過第六次獨立無紀錄稽核，過去拖住它的信任落差正在縮小。往下都穩。實用建議：純匿名選 Mullvad，全方位平衡選 Proton，最看重速度跟穩定串流就選 NordVPN。

en.commentary: Mullvad stays my number one because nobody does privacy-first the way it does, and RTINGS' latest testing lands in exactly the same place. You create an account with no email, no personal details, just a random number, and the company has publicly refused affiliate marketing and account-based tracking. At around five euros a month with a flat price for everyone, it is the rare product where the ethics and the value point the same direction. Proton VPN holds a close second and remains my pick for the person who wants that same privacy backbone plus a bigger server network, a polished app, and the best free tier in the business. Tom's Guide keeps handing it the editor nod and I understand why. NordVPN stays third and earns it on raw performance: it topped the recent speed tests, and its scam call protection has now rolled out across Europe, which is a genuine usability win that I bumped its score for. If you want the fastest big-name option and care less about the strict no-personal-info stance, Nord is the pick. My steady advice: Mullvad for principled privacy, Proton for the all-rounder, NordVPN for speed at scale.

zh-tw.commentary: Mullvad 穩坐我心中第一，因為隱私優先這件事沒人做得比它徹底，RTINGS 最新測試的結論也跟我完全一樣。你開帳號不用信箱、不用任何個資，就一組隨機號碼搞定，而且這家公司公開拒絕做聯盟行銷跟帳號追蹤。一個月大概五歐元，所有人同一個價，這是少數道德跟價值剛好指向同一邊的產品。Proton VPN 緊咬第二，我還是推薦給想要同樣隱私底子、又要更大伺服器網路、精緻 app 加上業界最強免費方案的人。Tom's Guide 一直把編輯首選頒給它，我很能理解。NordVPN 守住第三，靠的是硬實力，它在最近的速度測試拿下第一，而且防詐騙來電功能現在已經推廣到全歐洲，這是實打實的使用體驗提升，我也因此幫它加了點分。如果你想要大廠裡最快的那個，又沒那麼在意零個資這件事，選 Nord 就對了。我一貫的建議是，Mullvad 給重視原則隱私的人，Proton 是全能型首選，NordVPN 給追求大規模高速的人。

en.commentary: Mullvad holds my number one again, and the 2026 privacy comparisons I read this week keep landing on the same reasoning. Signup is the differentiator I care about most. Mullvad hands you a random account number with no email, no name, and lets you pay with cash mailed to Sweden or with crypto. That is provable minimal data retention, backed by eight or more audits from firms like Cure53 and NCC Group. If your priority is that nobody can tie the account to you, this is the one. Proton VPN stays second and remains the pick I recommend most often to normal users. It pairs strong privacy with genuinely fast speeds, and its Secure Core double hop routes traffic through Proton controlled locations for extra protection against exit server compromise. Five straight annual audits give me confidence in the no logs claim. NordVPN holds third as the feature and speed champion. It has been audited six times, posts the fastest connections in many tests, and carries the largest feature set for streaming and everyday use. My guidance stays clear. Go Mullvad if you are a privacy purist, Proton for strong privacy plus speed and a free tier, NordVPN when raw speed and streaming top your list. Scores hold steady this week since the audits reinforce the order rather than shift it.

zh-tw.commentary: Mullvad 這週再度守住我的第一名，我看的 2026 隱私比較文結論也都落在同一個理由上。註冊方式是我最在意的差別。Mullvad 直接給你一組隨機帳號，不用 email、不用姓名，還能用現金寄到瑞典或用加密貨幣付款。這就是可被證明的最小資料保留，背後有 Cure53 跟 NCC Group 這類公司做的八次以上稽核。如果你的首要目標是沒人能把帳號跟你連起來，選它就對了。Proton VPN 守住第二，也是我最常推給一般使用者的選擇。它把強隱私跟真正夠快的速度結合起來，Secure Core 雙跳把流量繞經 Proton 自己控管的節點，多一層防護對付出口節點被攻破的風險。連續五年的年度稽核讓我對它的無紀錄承諾有信心。NordVPN 守住第三，是功能跟速度的冠軍。它被稽核過六次，很多測試裡連線最快，串流跟日常使用的功能也最齊全。我的建議很清楚，隱私純粹派選 Mullvad，要強隱私加速度還有免費方案選 Proton，最看重純速度跟串流選 NordVPN。這週分數不動，因為這些稽核是在強化排序而不是撼動它。

highlights.title: Mullvad 還是隱私純粹派首選
highlights.body: 隨機帳號、不用 email、現金或加密貨幣付款，加上八次以上獨立稽核。不想讓帳號跟你有牽連，它就是第一。

highlights.title: Proton VPN 強隱私又夠快
highlights.body: Secure Core 雙跳加連續五年稽核，配上真的夠快的連線。我最常推給一般使用者的選擇。

en.commentary: Mullvad keeps my top spot because it remains the purest expression of what a privacy VPN should be. No email, a randomly generated account number, and payment options that include cash mailed to Sweden, this is anonymity by design, and this week's comparisons again called it the undisputed king of the category. That is exactly why it leads my ranking. Proton VPN holds second on the strongest privacy architecture on the board: Secure Core double-hop routing, five consecutive Securitum audits, and a SOC 2 Type II attestation. It costs some speed for that hardening, but for people who want a feature-rich privacy stack it is the most complete option. NordVPN sits third, and this week's coverage rightly praised its sixth independent no-logs audit and all-RAM fleet, with NordLynx retaining roughly 82 to 88 percent of baseline speed on nearby servers. For raw performance it leads, and its ranking reflects that. IVPN stays fourth as the quieter privacy purist for people who like Mullvad's ethos with a slightly different toolset. I held every score steady this week because the news was comparison analysis rather than audits or launches that change the picture. My guidance stands: pick Mullvad for anonymity, Proton for a full privacy suite, and Nord if speed leads your list.

en.commentary: Mullvad keeps my top spot for one clear reason, it is the most privacy preserving VPN you can pay for. This week's comparisons reaffirm the pitch, random account numbers with no email or name, and payment options that go as far as cash mailed to Sweden. If your top priority is minimal metadata, nothing else comes close, and the flat 5 euro price keeps it honest. Proton VPN holds second and I nudged its speed score up a tenth this week, because the 2026 testing keeps showing it as faster and more consistent than Mullvad while offering a far larger network of around 14,900 servers across 122 plus countries. It also carries the strongest streaming record of the three, so it is the pick for anyone who wants privacy plus everyday usefulness like Netflix and BBC iPlayer. NordVPN stays third and I lifted its speed a tenth as well, since reviewers clocked it at 418 to 440 Mbps on gigabit lines with post quantum encryption and a Deloitte verified no logs record. It is the choice when you want the fastest connection and the biggest feature set. IVPN and ExpressVPN hold their spots for the privacy purist and the polish seeker respectively. No rank swaps this week, the order is stable and the small speed bumps track the latest benchmark data.
